The '''Southern Jedi Ruins''' were the ruins of a [[Jedi enclave (Mustafar)/Legends|Jedi Enclave]] located on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]] within the region known as the [[Smoking Forest/Legends|Smoking Forest]].<ref name="SWG:ToO" /> The enclave was originally run by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] in some capacity, but was abandoned sometime prior to the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan]] during the [[New Sith Wars]].<ref name="JATMN">Daanat must have left the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]] and found the temple abandoned sometime prior to the Seventh Battle of Russan as the Brotherhood was entirely destroyed and Kaan was killed during the battle, meaning Daanat couldn't have left the brotherhood after this.</ref> The [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Vulta Daanat]] eventually found the abandoned temple after being forced to leave the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]] due to having different philosophies on [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] from the organization's leader, [[Skere Kaan]]. Having traveled [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] searching for Force-related artifacts, [[Gender/Legends|he]] settled down in the ruins and made them into the headquarters of his own Force-using organization, the [[Blackguard (Mustafar)/Legends|Blackguard]], becoming their first leader,<ref name="JATM">''[[Jedi Academy Training Manual]]''</ref> a role titled [[Kursk]].<ref name="SWG:ToO" />

By [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]] the Blackguard were still using the ruins as their headquarters, rarely leaving them except to recruit or search for further Force artifacts. The group's leader at the time, Kursk [[San'sii]], had begun recruiting non-Force-sensitive members into the Blackguard due to dwindling numbers, and these made up the bulk of the forces within the ruins. At this time a [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] [[Archaeology/Legends|archaeologist]] named [[Mi Fon Lu]] became interested in studying the ruins, but feared he would be attacked by the Blackguard inhabitants, and so hired several [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] to clear out the occupants. The spacers successfully killed all of the Blackguard within the ruins including San'sii and one of his strongest followers, [[Vansk]]. With the ruins clear, the Sullustan was able to move in and begin his research.<ref name="SWG:ToO" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Southern Jedi Ruins first appeared in the MMORPG ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'' as part of the [[Star Wars Galaxies: Trials of Obi-Wan|Trials of Obi-Wan]] expansion released in [[2005]]. In the expansion it was a location on the newly added [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]], which was home to the new Blackguard faction of enemies. Players took part in a chain of quests in which they were tasked with killing various Blackguard enemies within the ruins.

The Jedi ruins were later mentioned in the [[Wizards of the Coast]] [[Saga Edition]] [[Roleplaying game]] supplement book the ''[[Jedi Academy Training Manual]]'', released in [[2009]], where they were referenced as the home of the Blackguard in a section detailing alternate Force traditions.

''Star Wars Galaxies'' was shut down on [[December 15]], [[2011]].
